mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 6% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening demand for goods and services that reduces corporate profits and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in housing starts above 1.2 million signals an increase in supply that reduces housing prices and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in real GDP and a 3-month rise in the unemployment rate signal a weakening labor market and reduced consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with declining real GDP and rising unemployment are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising federal funds rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in initial jobless claims below 200,000 signals a strengthening labor market that increases consumer spending and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with declining initial jobless claims are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in real disposable personal income above 3% signals an increase in consumer spending that reduces corporate profits and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising real disposable personal income are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
